mission.title: Install and Configure Contour on Kubernetes
mission.description: Contour is a Kubernetes ingress controller that leverages the Envoy proxy to provide advanced routing capabilities. Installing Contour allows you to manage ingress traffic to your applications with enhanced features and dynamic configuration updates.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Bitnami Helm repository where the Contour chart is hosted.
```bash
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Contour using Helm
steps.description:
Install Contour with the specified version and create a namespace for it.
```bash
helm install contour bitnami/contour --version 21.1.4 --namespace contour --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Contour pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n contour
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the Contour deployment to set resource limits for better performance.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment contour --namespace contour --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Contour
steps.description:
Ensure that the necessary RBAC permissions are in place for Contour to function correctly. RBAC is typically configured automatically by the Helm chart. Verify RBAC is set up:
```bash
kubectl get clusterrolebindings | grep contour
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Contour release from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall contour --namespace contour
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) created by Contour.
```bash
kubectl delete crd httpproxies.projectcontour.io
kubectl delete crd ingressroutes.projectcontour.io
kubectl delete crd ingressroutesets.projectcontour.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Contour namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for Contour to clean up all associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace contour
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration of Contour before upgrading.
```bash
kubectl get all -n contour -o yaml > contour-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the Contour chart.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Contour
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Contour installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade contour bitnami/contour --version 21.1.4 --namespace contour
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Contour pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n contour
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If your Contour pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n contour
kubectl get nodes
```

troubleshooting.title: Ingress not routing traffic
troubleshooting.description:
If your ingress resources are not routing traffic, ensure that the Ingress or HTTPProxy resources are correctly configured.
```bash
kubectl get httpproxies -n contour
kubectl describe httpproxy <httpproxy-name> -n contour
```

troubleshooting.title: Contour not responding
troubleshooting.description:
If Contour is not responding, check the logs of the Contour pod for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <contour-pod-name> -n contour
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Contour will have all pods running in the 'contour' namespace, and you will be able to route traffic to your applications using the configured ingress resources. You can verify this by checking the status of the pods and ensuring they are in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
kubectl set resources deployment contour --namespace contour --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with RBAC enabled and the Helm CLI installed.
